revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '== 由緒 == [[福岡県]][[柳川市]]１４０年続く沖端水天宮は、明治２（[[１８６９]]）年に [[久留米水天宮]]から勧請神仏の分...'
revision.text:
== 由緒 == [[福岡県]][[柳川市]]１４０年続く沖端水天宮は、明治２（[[１８６９]]）年に [[久留米水天宮]]から勧請神仏の分霊を他の地に祭ること）され、 同地区内にあった[[稲荷神社]]、弥剣神社（[[祇園社]]）の３社を 合祀したもので、[[安徳天皇]]を祀る。 地元では「水天宮さん」と呼ばれている
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭 == 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。 *駐車場：なし
== 周辺情報 == *[[北原白秋]]生家
== 関連項目 ==
== 外部リンク == *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == [[福岡県]][[柳川市]]沖端町 １４０年続く沖端水天宮は、明治２（[[１８６９]]）年に [[久留米水天宮]]から勧請神仏の分霊を他の地に祭ること）され、 同地区内にあった[[稲荷神社]]、弥剣神社（[[祇園社]]）の３社を 合祀したもので、[[安徳天皇]]を祀る。 地元では「水天宮さん」と呼ばれている
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭 == 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。 *駐車場：なし
== 周辺情報 == *[[北原白秋]]生家
== 関連項目 ==
== 外部リンク == *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == [[福岡県]][[柳川市]]沖端町に 天正１５年（１５８７年）[[稲荷社]]が立花宗茂公によってまつられ、 [[祇園社]]が文化年間（１８０４～１８１７年）に合祀、、[[水天宮]]は明治２年[[久留米水天宮]]総本社の分霊を合祀したもので、祭神は[[安徳天皇]]を祀る。 地元では「水天宮さん」と呼ばれている。5月3,4,5日に行われる大祭には三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭 == 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。 *駐車場：なし
== 周辺情報 == *[[北原白秋]]生家
== 関連項目 ==
== 外部リンク == *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == [[福岡県]][[柳川市]]沖端町に 天正１５年（１５８７年）[[稲荷社]]が[[立花宗茂]]公によってまつられ、 [[祇園社]]が文化年間（１８０４～１８１７年）に合祀、[[水天宮]]は明治２年[[久留米水天宮]]総本社の分霊を合祀したもので祭神は[[安徳天皇]]を祀る。 地元では「水天宮さん」と呼ばれている。5月3,4,5日に行われる大祭には三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭 == 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。 *駐車場：なし
== 周辺情報 == *[[北原白秋]]生家
== 関連項目 ==
== 外部リンク == *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == [[福岡県]][[柳川市]]沖端町に 天正１５年（１５８７年）[[稲荷社]]が[[立花宗茂]]公によってまつられ、 [[祇園社]]が文化年間（１８０４～１８１７年）に合祀、[[水天宮]]は明治２年[[久留米水天宮]]総本社の分霊を合祀したもので祭神は[[安徳天皇]]を祀る。 地元では「水天宮さん」と呼ばれている。5月3,4,5日に行われる沖端水天宮祭りには三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭 == 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。 *駐車場：なし

{{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == [[福岡県]][[柳川市]]沖端町に 天正15年（1587年）[[稲荷社]]が[[立花宗茂]]公によってまつられ、 [[祇園社]]が文化年間（1804～1817年）に合祀、[[水天宮]]は明治2年[[久留米水天宮]]総本社の分霊を合祀したもので祭神は[[安徳天皇]]を祀る。 地元では「水天宮さん」と呼ばれている。5月3,4,5日に行われる沖端水天宮祭りには三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭
== 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。 *駐車場：なし

revision.comment: 定義調整等
revision.text:
'''沖端水天宮'''（おきのはたすいてんぐう）は[[福岡県]][[柳川市]]沖端町にある神社（[[水天宮]]）。
== 由緒 == [[文禄]]3年（[[1587年]]）に[[立花宗茂]]により[[稲荷神社]]として建立され、祇園社は[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間（1804～1818年）に京都弥剣神社の分霊が合祀されたものである。水天宮は明治2年（1869年）に[[久留米水天宮]]総本社の分霊を合祀したもので、祭神は[[安徳天皇]]を祀る。
地元では「水天宮さん」と呼ばれている。5月3・4・5日に行われる沖端水天宮祭りには三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される。
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭
== 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。 *駐車場：なし
== 外部リンク == *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おきのはたすいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 情報函設置他
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 沖端水天宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 福岡県柳川市稲荷町 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 安徳天皇 |社格 = |創建 = 明治2年（1869年） |本殿 = |別名 = 水天宮さん |札所 = |例祭 = 5月3-5日 |神事 = }}
'''沖端水天宮'''（おきのはたすいてんぐう）は[[福岡県]][[柳川市]]稲荷町にある[[水天宮|神社（水天宮）]]。
== 祭神 == [[安徳天皇]]
== 由緒 == [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に[[本社|総本社]]である[[久留米水天宮]]の[[分霊]]を[[勧請]]、同地区内にあった稲荷神社と弥剣神社（祇園社）を[[合祀]]して創祀された。なお、稲荷神社は[[文禄]]3年（[[1587年]]）に[[立花宗茂]]により創祀された神社、弥剣神社は[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間（1804 - 1818年）の創祀。
地元では「'''水天宮さん'''」と呼ばれている。5月3・4・5日に行われる沖端水天宮祭りには三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される。
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭
== 交通 == *[[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 :[[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。
== 外部リンク == *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おきのはたすいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:水天宮|福岡]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''沖端水天宮'''（おきのはたすいてんぐう）は[[福岡県]][[柳川市]]稲荷町にある[[水天宮|神社（水天宮）]]。
== 祭神 == [[安徳天皇]]
== 由緒 == [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に[[本社|総本社]]である[[久留米水天宮]]の[[分霊]]を[[勧請]]、同地区内にあった稲荷神社と弥剣神社（祇園社）を[[合祀]]して創祀された。なお、稲荷神社は[[文禄]]3年（[[1587年]]）に[[立花宗茂]]により創祀された神社、弥剣神社は[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間（1804 - 1818年）の創祀。
地元では「'''水天宮さん'''」と呼ばれている。5月3・4・5日に行われる沖端水天宮祭りには三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される。
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭
== 交通 == * [[西日本鉄道]]（西鉄）[[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅]]より徒歩40分 : [[西鉄柳川駅]]からは[[西鉄バス]]御花・沖端行 御花前バス停から徒歩5分。

revision.comment: 交通アクセス修正・追記
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 沖端水天宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 福岡県柳川市稲荷町 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 安徳天皇 |社格 = |創建 = 明治2年（1869年） |本殿 = |別名 = 水天宮さん |札所 = |例祭 = 5月3-5日 |神事 = }}
'''沖端水天宮'''（おきのはたすいてんぐう）は[[福岡県]][[柳川市]]稲荷町にある[[水天宮|神社（水天宮）]]。
== 祭神 == [[安徳天皇]]
== 由緒 == [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に[[本社|総本社]]である[[久留米水天宮]]の[[分霊]]を[[勧請]]、同地区内にあった稲荷神社と弥剣神社（祇園社）を[[合祀]]して創祀された。なお、稲荷神社は[[文禄]]3年（[[1587年]]）に[[立花宗茂]]により創祀された神社、弥剣神社は[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間（1804 - 1818年）の創祀。
地元では「'''水天宮さん'''」と呼ばれている。5月3・4・5日に行われる沖端水天宮祭りには三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される。
== 祭事 == *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭
== 交通アクセス == *[[西日本鉄道|西鉄]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅|柳川駅]]下車の上、 **[[西鉄バス]]「早津江」行きに乗車、「水天宮入口」バス停にて下車、徒歩4分 **同バス「早津江」行き、または「柳川病院循環」に乗車、「御花前」バス停にて下車、徒歩6分 **[[堀川バス]]「かんぽの宿柳川」行きに乗車、終点の同バス停にて下車、徒歩8分 **同駅よりタクシーで約10分
== 外部リンク == *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おきのはたすいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:水天宮|福岡]] [[Category:柳川市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 交通アクセス == *[[西日本鉄道|西鉄]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅|柳川駅]]下車の上、 **[[西鉄バス]]「早津江」行きに乗車、「水天宮入口」バス停にて下車、徒歩4分 **同バス「早津江」行き、または「柳川病院循環」に乗車、「御花前」バス停にて下車、徒歩6分 **[[堀川バス]]「かんぽの宿柳川」行きに乗車、終点の同バス停にて下車、徒歩8分 **同駅よりタクシーで約10分

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 水天宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 福岡県柳川市稲荷町 |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 安徳天皇 |社格 = |創建 = 明治2年（1869年） |本殿 = |別名 = 沖端水天宮、水天宮さん |札所 = |例祭 = 5月3-5日 |神事 = }} '''沖端水天宮'''（おきのはたすいてんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[柳川市]]稲荷町にある[[水天宮|神社（水天宮）]]。[[登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''水天宮'''（すいてんぐう）。
== 祭神 == *[[安徳天皇]]
== 由緒 == [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に[[本社|総本社]]である[[水天宮 (久留米市)|久留米水天宮]]の[[分霊]]を[[勧請]]、同地区内にあった[[稲荷神社]]と弥剣神社（[[祇園社]]）を[[合祀]]して創祀された。なお、稲荷神社は[[文禄]]3年（[[1587年]]）に[[立花宗茂]]により創祀された神社、弥剣神社は[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間（1804 - 1818年）の創祀。
地元では「'''水天宮さん'''」と呼ばれている。5月3・4・5日に行われる沖端水天宮祭りには三神丸という舟舞台が作られ夕刻より水天宮囃子が演奏される。
== 祭事 == [[ファイル:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] *2月初午日 初午祭 *5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 *7月20・21日 祇園祭
== 交通アクセス == *[[西日本鉄道|西鉄]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄柳川駅|柳川駅]]下車の上、 **[[西鉄バス]]「早津江」行きに乗車、「水天宮入口」バス停にて下車、徒歩4分 **同バス「早津江」行き、または「柳川病院循環」に乗車、「御花前」バス停にて下車、徒歩6分 **[[堀川バス]]「かんぽの宿柳川」行きに乗車、終点の同バス停にて下車、徒歩8分 **同駅よりタクシーで約10分
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おきのはたすいてんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:水天宮]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:柳川市の歴史]] [[Category:柳川市の建築物]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11552577]] Wikidata name: Okinohata Suitengū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |Status = |Founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|qid=Q585630}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|qid=Q316651}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|qq=|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|qq=|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{Myōken Faith}}

[[ja:沖端水天宮]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = {{ill|File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|ja|image:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|lt=300px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|qq=|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|qq=|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == {{ill|File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|ja|image:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|lt=thumb|extra=Suitengu Grand Festival|extra_ja=水天宮大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|qq=|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11552577]] Wikidata name: Okinohata Suitengū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |Status = |Founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|qid=Q585630}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|qid=Q316651}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|qq=|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|qq=|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{Myōken Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = {{ill|File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|ja|image:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|lt=300px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|qq=|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|qq=|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == {{ill|File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|ja|image:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|lt=thumb|extra=Suitengu Grand Festival|extra_ja=水天宮大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|qq=|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|qq=|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|qq=|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|qq=|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == {{ill|File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|ja|image:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|lt=thumb|extra=Suitengu Grand Festival|extra_ja=水天宮大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|qq=|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == {{ill|File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|ja|image:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|lt=thumb|extra=Suitengu Grand Festival|extra_ja=水天宮大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[Category:Pages with files linked by ill]]{{ill|File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|ja|image:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|lt=thumb|extra=Suitengu Grand Festival|extra_ja=水天宮大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11552577]] Wikidata name: Okinohata Suitengū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |Status = |Founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{Myōken Faith}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).

== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11552577]] Wikidata name: Okinohata Suitengū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |Status = |Founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11552577]] Wikidata name: Okinohata Suitengū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |Status = |Founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
