'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inari Town, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The registered [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]] name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== 由緒 == [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) saw the enshrinement of the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]] from the head office – [[:en:Corporate headquarters|General Head Office]] – of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]], which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while the Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]][[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]][[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]] – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{Myōken Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:沖端水天宮]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a [[:en:Suitengū|Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)]] located in Inarimachi, [[:en:Yanagawa, Fukuoka|Yanagawa City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. According to [[:en:Civil registration|Registration]], the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]]
== Origins == In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a divine fragment ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the head shrine ([[:en:Corporate headquarters|Head Shrine]]) of [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Kurume Suitengu]] was enshrined by means of enshrinement ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ([[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku Era]] 3 ([[:en:1587|1587]]) by [[:en:Tachibana Muneshige|Tachibana Muneshige]], while Yaken Shrine was established during the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishitetsu]], [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]], and [[:en:Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|Yanagawa Station]]; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)}} located in Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The registered {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration}} name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}
== 由緒 == {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) saw the enshrinement of the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}} from the head office – {{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=General Head Office}} – of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu}}, which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while the Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station}} – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{Myōken Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:沖端水天宮]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}} |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)}} located in Inarimachi, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. According to {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration}}, the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}
== Origins == In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}), a divine fragment ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}) from the head shrine ({{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=Head Shrine}}) of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu}} was enshrined by means of enshrinement ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku Era}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]]
{{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}} |Status = |Founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)}} located in Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The registered {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration}} name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}
== 由緒 == {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) saw the enshrinement of the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}} from the head office – {{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=General Head Office}} – of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu}}, which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while the Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station}} – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{Myōken Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:沖端水天宮]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}} |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)}} located in Inarimachi, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. According to {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration}}, the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}
== Origins == In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}), a divine fragment ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}) from the head shrine ({{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=Head Shrine}}) of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu}} was enshrined by means of enshrinement ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku Era}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]]
{{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}} |Status = |Founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)}} located in Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The registered {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration}} name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}
== 由緒 == {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) saw the enshrinement of the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}} from the head office – {{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=General Head Office}} – of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu}}, which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while the Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station}} – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4‑minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6‑minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8‑minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

[[ja:沖端水天宮]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Inarimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}} |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu‑san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)}} located in Inarimachi, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. According to {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration}}, the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}
== Origins == In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}), a divine fragment ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}) from the head shrine ({{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=Head Shrine}}) of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu}} was enshrined by means of enshrinement ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku Era}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu‑san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4‑minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6‑minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8‑minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4-minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6-minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8-minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]]
{{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}} |Status = |Founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)|1=Suitengū}} located in Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The registered {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}} name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== 由緒 == {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) saw the enshrinement of the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}} from the head office – {{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=General Head Office|1=Corporate headquarters}} – of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}}, which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while the Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Ōmuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}} – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4-minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6-minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8-minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)|1=Suitengū}} located in Inarimachi, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. According to {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}}, the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== Origins == In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a divine fragment ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}) from the head shrine ({{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=Head Shrine|1=Corporate headquarters}}) of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}} was enshrined by means of enshrinement ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}}); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku Era|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu-san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4-minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6-minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8-minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

According to {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}}, the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== Origins == In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a divine fragment ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}) from the head shrine ({{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=Head Shrine|1=Corporate headquarters}}) of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}} was enshrined by means of enshrinement ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}}); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku Era|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu-san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4-minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6-minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8-minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okinohata-suitengu]][[ja:沖端水天宮]]
{{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}} |Status = |Founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)|1=Suitengū}} located in Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The registered {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}} name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== 由緒 == {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) saw the enshrinement of the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}} from the head office – {{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=General Head Office|1=Corporate headquarters}} – of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}}, which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while the Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Ōmuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}} – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4-minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6-minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8-minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

== Access == * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Ōmuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}} – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4-minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6-minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8-minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.

[[ja:沖端水天宮]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Q11596874|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}} |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)|1=Suitengū}} located in Inarimachi, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. According to {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}}, the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== Origins == In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a divine fragment ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}) from the head shrine ({{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=Head Shrine|1=Corporate headquarters}}) of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}} was enshrined by means of enshrinement ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}}); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku Era|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu-san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4-minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6-minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8-minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |image = File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg |Location = Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}} |Status = |Founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |Votive Site = |Annual Festival = May 3–5 |Shinto Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Yanagawa|WD=Q585630|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.15875|N|130.395139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Antoku|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}} }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)|1=Suitengū}} located in Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The registered {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}} name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== 由緒 == {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) saw the enshrinement of the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}} from the head office – {{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=General Head Office|1=Corporate headquarters}} – of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}}, which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while the Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭

'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (沖端水天宮) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)|1=Suitengū}} located in Inari Town, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The registered {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}} name under the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== 由緒 == {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) saw the enshrinement of the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}} from the head office – {{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=General Head Office|1=Corporate headquarters}} – of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}}, which was combined ({{ill|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/合祀|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社（{{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}）) within the area to establish the shrine. In addition, the Inari Shrine was founded in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while the Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} period (1804–1818).
Locally, it is known as "'''Suitengu-san'''". During the Okinohata Suitengu Festival held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage called Sangu-maru is constructed and the Suitengu festival music is performed from dusk.
== 祭事 == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb|水天宮大祭]] * 2月初午日 初午祭 * 5月3～5日 水天宮大祭 * 7月20・21日 祇園祭
== Access == * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Ōmuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}{{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}} – alight and then: ** Board the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for "Hayatsue" ("早津江"), alight at the "Suitengu Entrance" ("水天宮入口") bus stop; a 4-minute walk. ** Alternatively, take the same bus bound for "Hayatsue" or the "Yanagawa Hospital Loop" ("柳川病院循環"), alight at the "Mihanae" ("御花前") bus stop; a 6-minute walk. ** Board the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for "Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa" ("かんぽの宿柳川"), alight at the final stop; an 8-minute walk. ** Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} *[http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ 柳川市沖端水天宮]（沖端町青年部） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

[[ja:沖端水天宮]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Suitengu (水天宮) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Okinohata-suitengu02n.jpg|thumb]] |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Inarimachi|ja|稲荷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarimachi|1=Q11596874|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596874}} |位置 = {{Coord|33|09|31.5|N|130|23|42.5|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}} |type = |Founding = Meiji 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) |本殿 = |Alternative names = Okinohata Suitengu, Suitengu-san |札所 = |festival = May 3–5 |神事 = }}
'''Okinohata Suitengu''' (おきのはたすいてんぐう) is a {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Shinto Shrine (Suitengu)|1=Suitengū}} located in Inarimachi, {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa City|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. According to {{ill|Civil registration|en|Civil registration|WD=Q83708009|lt=Registration|1=Civil registration}}, the registered name of the {{ill|Religious Corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporation|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7311343}} is '''Suitengu''' (すいてんぐう).
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}
== Origins == In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a divine fragment ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}) from the head shrine ({{ill|Corporate headquarters|en|Corporate headquarters|WD=Q1530704|lt=Head Shrine|1=Corporate headquarters}}) of {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Kurume Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}} was enshrined by means of enshrinement ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}}); at the same time, the Inari Shrine ({{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}) and Yaken Shrine (弥剣神社 ({{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}})) that existed in the area were combined ({{ill|Combined Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Combined Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) and established. Note that Inari Shrine was established in {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku Era|1=Bunroku}} 3 ({{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}}) by {{ill|Tachibana Muneshige|en|Tachibana Muneshige|WD=Q1071770|lt=Tachibana Muneshige|1=Tachibana Muneshige}}, while Yaken Shrine was established during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818).
Locally, it is called '''Suitengu-san'''. At the Okinohata Suitengu Festival, held on May 3, 4, and 5, a boat stage known as Sanjinmaru is constructed, and from dusk onwards, Suitengu festival music is performed.
== Festivals == [[File:Fukuoka Yanagawa10s3.jpg|thumb]] * On the first Horse Day of February: Hatsuuma Festival * May 3–5: Suitengu Grand Festival * July 20–21: Gion Festival
== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4-minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6-minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8-minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Okinohata-suitengu}} * [http://sagemon.net/suitengu/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|ja|柳川市沖端水天宮|lt=Okinohata Suitengu of Yanagawa|lt_ja=沖端水天宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagawa City Okinohata Suitengu|qq=}}] (Okinohata Town Youth Division) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|沖端水天宮|version=83992659|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Access == * Disembark at {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishitetsu|1=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}}, {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|1=Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line}}, and {{ill|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|en|Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station|WD=Q11629907|lt=Yanagawa Station|1=Nishitetsu Yanagawa Station}}; then take the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} bound for “Hayatsue” and alight at the “Suitengu Entrance” bus stop (a 4-minute walk). * Alternatively, board the same “Hayatsue” bus or the “Yanagawa Hospital Loop” and alight at the “Gohanae” bus stop (a 6-minute walk). * Or, take the {{ill|Horikawa Bus|ja|堀川バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Bus|1=Horikawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427415}} bound for “Kanpo no Yado Yanagawa” and alight at its terminal stop (an 8-minute walk). * Approximately 10 minutes by taxi from the station.
